content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The biggest productivity killer on your computer isn’t social media
It's tab switching.
It happens so often...
You barely notice it.
Write a sentence.
Switch tabs.
Check your tasks.
Switch tabs.
Open your notes.
Switch tabs.
Search for a file.
Switch tabs.
Reply to a message.
Switch tabs.
By lunchtime...
You've switched dozens of times.
By the end of the week...
Probably hundreds.
Most people think those few seconds don't matter.
They're wrong.
The real cost isn't the click.
It's what happens to your brain.
Every time you jump between apps...
Your focus has to restart.
Your train of thought gets interrupted.
Your momentum disappears.
It's like trying to read a book...
While someone changes the page every thirty seconds.
Eventually, you stop thinking deeply.
You start reacting.
That's how busy days happen.
You answer messages.
Update tasks.
Move files around.
Yet somehow...
The important work never gets finished.
I used to think I needed more discipline.
Turns out...
I needed fewer places to work.
Instead of adding another app...
I started removing them.
The fewer tabs I opened...
The easier it became to stay focused.
That's what eventually led me to Taskade.
Not because it promised to make me work harder.
Because it let me work in one place.
Projects.
Notes.
AI.
Planning.
Tasks.
Workflows.
Connected.
Less switching.
More creating.
That simple change gave me something I hadn't had in a long time...
Uninterrupted thinking.
And that's where the best work happens.
If you've been wondering why your days feel so busy...
It might not be your workload.
It might be your workspace.
If you’d like to see the workspace that helped me simplify everything, take a look here.
Sometimes the fastest way to get more done...
Isn't working faster.
It's switching less.
Disclosure: This post contains an affiliate link.
